programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: Both sides in Alex Murdaugh's murder trial argue about the issue of infidelity.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: The lead investigator who looked into the murders of Maggie and Paul Murdaugh takes the stand.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: Investigators working the double homicide of Maggie and Paul Murdaugh also looked into the fatal boat crash that Paul was accused in, which was a big part of this case.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: The jury watched an unedited version of Alex Murdaugh's final police interview. It lasted an hour and a half. And it included questions about Alex's alibi for the time Maggie and Paul were killed.

programme.title: SC v. Alex Murdaugh
programme.desc: David Owen was the lead investigator and helped the prosecution button up its case.

programme.title: Suitcase Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors listened to the 911 operator walk Sarah Boone performing CPR on Jorge Torres. This was just minutes after opening up the suitcase Torres was zipped up in and finding him unresponsive. Prosecutors introduced the jury to bodycam footage of police arriving on the scene and questioning Boone.

programme.title: Suitcase Murder Trial
programme.desc: The unedited version of the police bodycam questioning Sarah Boone on what happened played in court. Prosecutors claimed she intentionally killed her boyfriend, Jorge Torres, using a suitcase in a deadly game of hide-and-seek. They introduced police bodycam video from two responding officers to the crime scene. At the time of his death Torres' height was five feet three inches, and he weighed only 103 pounds.

programme.title: Suitcase Murder Trial
programme.desc: Crime scene photos displayed for jurors. As officers gathered information and collected key evidence they were learning about the alcohol-induced game of hide-and-seek that led to Jorge Torres death. Cross-examination began on the crime scene investigator who collected and processed key evidence. The state was trying to prove that Boone intentionally killed her boyfriend, Jorge Torres, when she zipped up the suitcase he got into before going off to bed.

programme.title: Suitcase Murder Trial
programme.desc: The State's medical examiner took the stand. Dr. Sara Zydowicz conducted the autopsy of Jorge Torres. Prosecutors told jurors Torres died of environmental suffocation when he lay in a suitcase for at least 11 hours. Next, the jury shifted their attention to the physical evidence prosecutors believed proved Boone killed her boyfriend. This testimony came from the DNA analyst assigned to this case.

programme.title: Burn Pile Murder Trial
programme.desc: According to the defendant's oldest adult child, family tension was brewing in the weeks leading up to Gary Farris' death. This family tension involved accusations of mischief with finances and spousal cheating. Gary went missing out of the blue during the July 4th holiday. All of his four adult children were panicking and went on a search. Unbeknownst to them at the time, their father was shot to death at home and his remains were burnt up and buried outside.

programme.title: Burn Pile Murder Trial
programme.desc: Melody Farris' oldest son learned how awful it was to find out that his dad, Gary, was dead and his remains were found in a burn pile on the family farm. Chris Farris gave the jury his memory of events that led up to that tragic discovery. After Gary's remains were discovered and the police began their investigation, confusion was the first to set in among his four adult children and then suspicions arose.

programme.title: Burn Pile Murder Trial
programme.desc: Chris Farris told jurors that he and his siblings were frantic and confused after their father's remains were found. As the oldest of the children, Chris was given the role of the temporary administrator of his father, Gary's estate. On the stand, he told the jury that his father's accounts were all frozen during the police investigation.

programme.title: Burn Pile Murder Trial
programme.desc: Cross-examination began on the oldest child of Gary and Melody Farris. Melody's defense team wanted to poke holes in the family drama which the State claims was the motive for her to kill her husband, Gary, of 38 years. But, things got personal with Chris Farris on the stand. He admitted that he struggled with alcohol addiction which affected his relationship with his family.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Violence shattered a quiet Texas neighborhood in 1988 when a mother and child are kidnapped and murdered along with a bystander. In their search for drug trafficker and killer Genaro Ruiz Camacho, the FBI exposed a drug cartel that flourished.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When notorious bank robber Byron Chubbuck escaped from New Mexico State Penitentiary in 2000, he almost immediately began stealing again. The FBI teamed up with U.S. Marshals to capture a resourceful criminal who proved he would never stop fighting.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1982, a California highway patrol officer pulled a young woman over for speeding. He bullied her into sex and then killed her. He then used a decade of police experience to try to cover up his crime and convince investigators of his innocence.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When an 80-year-old woman is found barely conscious next to the body of her murdered son in an abandoned van, the FBI was brought in and linked the crime to a teenager and middle-aged man.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: No suspect had emerged after the rapes and murders of ten women near Tampa, Florida in 1984. The killer was a ruthless and terrifying predator, the newest member of an infamous group known as "serial killers."

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1986, a psychotic killer shot and killed a police officer at point-blank range, leading investigators in a wild chase across the Midwest. As his tally of murders and abductions grew, the FBI raced against time to put a stop to the deranged gunman.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Angered by the government's policy in the Middle East, Mir Qazi brutally gunned down two members of the CIA as they waited in their cars at a stoplight. The killer fled the country and was placed on the FBI Ten Most Wanted Fugitives list.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1989, a quiet community in Ohio was shattered when a sniper gunned down an outdoorsman without reason.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A sniper murdered two Black men as they jogged with two white women. The FBI placed their suspect on their Ten Most Wanted list and began an intense manhunt. What followed was the arrest, trial and conviction of a white supremacist serial killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1980s, as drug profits grew, gangs ruled the streets of Chicago. Led by mastermind Jeff Fort, the dangerous, power-hungry El Rukn street gang attempted to conspire with Libya to perform acts of domestic terrorism for money.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: New Jersey millionaire Frank Black flew to West Palm Beach, Florida to close a major business deal but never returned home. When the FBI was called in, agents learned of a mystery woman whom Black met while on his trip.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A class field trip ended with an 11-year-old girl missing. When the parents failed to locate their daughter in their quiet Illinois town, the FBI joined the investigation, working with friends and family in an urgent search to find the missing girl.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: FBI investigations of alleged corruption in the early 1990s grew to include the 1994 murder of Kim Groves.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The FBI began an undercover operation to expose the Chicago Mafia's infiltration into the city's surrounding suburbs. The investigation resulted in the arrests of former police chief Michael J. Corbitt and Alan Masters for murder and racketeering.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The Los Macheteros terrorist organization sought to end U.S. rule of Puerto Rico through violence. When the group claimed responsibility for an attack on 18 unarmed servicemen traveling near San Juan, the FBI is called in to investigate the killers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A series of brutal bank robberies and murders led to an exhaustive eight-month investigation by the FBI.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990s Philadelphia, a ruthless grab for power led to a full-scale mob war. Using informants and high-tech surveillance, the FBI infiltrated the most powerful mob family in the city and sought to bring down the organization.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A convicted killer serving life for manslaughter is found to be guilty of a second murder 32 years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Victor Farrant is a convicted rapist who murdered his former girlfriend in 1996 and attempted to murder another woman after he was released from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the Baton Rouge Serial Killer, known for murdering seven women in the area and sentenced to death for his chilling crimes.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of a terrifying killer who snatched away the lives of four victims before being caught while trying to claim his fifth.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of serial killer Geoffrey Evans and his accomplice John Shaw, who hit headlines in 1976 after the murders of two women in Ireland.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In April 1997, 27-year-old Rachel McGrath was stabbed to death by an unknown attacker in a car park. 24 hours later and 100 miles away, a terrified 17-year-old student flagged down a motorist, telling them she'd just escaped from a man.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of Lee Ford, who killed his wife and four stepchildren in a cold-blooded murder spree over 24 hours in September 2000.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the serial killer who brutally snatched away the lives of eight innocent women during a decade-long killing spree in Louisiana.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: She became the first woman to be executed by lethal injection after taking six lives. What drove her to commit these callous murders?

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of Mark Martin, who murdered three homeless women in his quest to become Nottingham's first serial killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Paul Runge raped and killed 6 women and a young girl in Chicago, Illinois. The Police had suspected him in the early investigation but lacked the evidence to make an arrest.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of 27-year-old Pawel Relowicz, who raped and killed 21-year-old Hull student Libby Squire in a case that made headlines around the UK.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Jerome Brudos was a serial killer and necrophile who murdered four women in Oregon during the 1960s. He was known as the 'The Lust Killer' and 'The Shoe Fetish Slayer.'

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Wayne Adam Ford is an American serial killer who strangled and dismembered three of his four victims. The first body was found in October 1997 and had been so thoroughly dismembered that her identity remains unknown.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Remorseless murderer Casey Pigge's main ambition is to be the ultimate serial killer. If unrestrained, he remains a deadly threat.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Being behind bars only made Italian-American mobster Freddy Geas even more brutal.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Feared Aryan Warriors gang leader 'Mugsy' Williams was a one-man crimewave. His violence ultimately sparked a bloody showdown in a Nevada jail, leaving multiple inmates dead.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Convicted of shooting a man and 21 other felonies, Miti Maugaotega Jr. receives one of the longest prison sentences in Hawaiʻi history. Behind bars, he joins a prison gang and becomes a tornado of death and destruction.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the cold-blooded serial killer who took the lives of eleven people during a terrifying decade-long murder campaign.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of an Air Force officer was found dead in her bed with a plastic laundry bag near her face. Further investigation proved that the scene had been staged. Her death wasn't an accident, it was cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young boy went missing, police organized the largest search in the history of their small town.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A patrolman was dispatched to what he thought would be a routine traffic call until he looked in the car.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The decomposed body of a young woman was discovered in a Bakersfield irrigation canal. If there was trace evidence, it had been washed away.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A behavioral profile caused the Baton Rouge Police Department to search for the wrong man. They might not have made an arrest had it not been for a DNA picture of the suspect painted by a molecular biologist.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An obstetrician returned home from the hospital and found his wife on the bathroom floor. She was covered with blood and not breathing. He tried unsuccessfully to revive her, staining his clothes with her blood in the process, and then he called 911.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A California teen went missing. Police suspected she'd run away until her body was discovered in a ditch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police recovered the car of a missing man, they expected to find his body - but it wasn't there.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Emergency dispatch received a call from a man who said his girlfriend shot and killed herself. The autopsy revealed that the gunshot wound was not self-inflicted and the evidence on her body would give police a golden opportunity to catch her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Native American woman was brutally killed in the desert of New Mexico, and the crime scene was rich in forensic evidence: tire tracks, shoe impressions and even the murder weapons.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A brilliant young architect became ill and died just before she was to testify in a criminal trial. The autopsy revealed she'd been poisoned with arsenic. Investigators had to determine which person who knew her had a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An employee of a dry cleaner was raped and murdered in the store. Police turned to forensic science for the answers they needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of a millionaire indicated robbery, but investigators wondered if there was something more.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young woman disappeared, police feared she was the latest victim in a string of similar crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A car carrying three men pulls up alongside another on an Alaskan highway and fires shots, leaving a passenger dead. One of the passengers in the killer's car agrees to testify against his friends. The resulting trials don't end the carnage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A college student was found dead, and the evidence suggested he knew his killer. Three hairs and some microscopic cells helped police to unravel a web of lies, and find the motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In an affluent suburb, police were called to the scene of what appeared to be an accidental drowning.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When two women from the same town were murdered in the same way, police feared a serial killer was on the loose. At first they thought the victims had nothing in common until they found tiny clues linking them to the same man.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was found dead on a golf course. The grass on the course was so distinctive, it had evidentiary value.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teenager went missing after an evening of horseback riding. Her body was found a month later, three miles from her home. The killer unknowingly left trace evidence behind - tiny but unmistakable clues which pointed to him and him alone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a popular disc jockey was found murdered in a community garden, police swung into action. A sniffer dog and a blood spatter expert led police to the killer and he'd been much closer than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a man is shot down in his garage, police discover that a wound on the widow may have been self-inflicted.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three seemingly unrelated deaths proved to be serial murders. The killer used poison which had no taste.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a victim is brutally murdered in his bed, investigators find a shoe impression in the mud outside.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police hoped the shoe impressions found at a crime scene would put their investigation back on track.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the head chef of a historic Philadelphia restaurant was found dead, investigators interviewed the usual suspects: family, friends and coworkers. As they sifted through the evidence, police uncovered a chilling tale of debt and deceit.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial arsonist was on the loose. It was up to forensic scientists to find the clues that lay in the ashes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Genell Plude is found dead in her bathroom, the scene points to suicide. But a coroner's inquest and a unique application of forensic science gave investigators a different explanation for her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three homicides on two continents looked like professional executions. Investigators on both sides of the Atlantic needed to find out if they were related and, if they were, who or what they had in common.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 29-year-old woman was killed instantly when a bomb exploded in her home. The device was so powerful that shrapnel was embedded in houses across the street. The bomber had not only knowledge and skill, but also a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A killer tried to incinerate and destroy everything that could link him to his crime. But in doing so, he inadvertently created new forensic evidence, evidence which came to light with a technique never before used in a criminal investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman appears to have lost control of her SUV and plunged down an embankment into a shallow creek. But investigators suspect foul play when they find snowy footprints leading from the crime scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Russian immigrant is found dead and a strange phone call to his office is the only lead investigators have.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The prime suspect had a criminal record and his driver's license was found at the scene of a brutal double homicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a little girl got sick and died, investigators were stumped. Was it an accident, an unexplained illness or murder?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a car was found in a drainage ditch with two bodies inside, a fingertip torn from a latex glove would point investigators to both the crime scene and the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of a World War II veteran slowed to a halt when the prime suspect had a solid alibi. But a lucky break led to a shady character who wore distinctive boots.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies respond to a report of an inebriated man trying to change a tire on the side of the road. Police investigate a report of a suspicious person in the back of a store loading items onto his truck.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A 9-1-1 call about a man threatening to kill his family. The investigation of a shoplifting case leads police to over 66 grams of black tar heroin. A domestic violence call.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A woman's boyfriend is arrested for domestic violence. Police search for two missing tourists. A naked transient occupies a supermarket bathroom for over an hour.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: A Big Dog & Bunches of Meth

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Follow a SWAT mission from briefing to execution. A man wanted for assault with a deadly weapon is taken into custody.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: We follow Deputy Bryan Payne up close and personal on his night shift as he goes about a typical day for a deputy in El Dorado County.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police seek to take down a known drug house where they expect many arrests. Deputies investigate a van parked after hours in a school parking area. Knives, bullets and marijuana are found in a suspicious car.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: A Day at the Drug House
programme.desc: A deputy follows two probationers into a known drug house. A water company worker contacting people about a water issue calls police about an extremely inebriated woman who fell down.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputy responds to a report of shoplifting. The search of a motel room yields incriminating evidence.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An anonymous caller gives the whereabouts of a stolen vehicle. The driver of a car stopped at an intersection is unresponsive. A deputy responds to a report of a vehicle that may have been used in an earlier crime.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A traffic stop reveals a man with stolen food and burglary tools who drives without a license. A deputy responds to a call about two intoxicated men. Two men are reportedly fighting at a department store.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police search for a robbery suspect who has a felony warrant for his arrest. Deputies respond to a call from a mother who claims her son is attacking her. A traffic stop leads to a drug possession charge.

programme.desc: A domestic violence call leads to a foot chase. Deputies respond to a burglary in progress. A deputy finds heroin on a man who was stumbling in the road.

programme.desc: Police execute a search warrant on a marijuana grow operation run by illegal Mexican nationals. A person reports they found three pipe bombs. In a domestic violence call, a man is reported to be burning all his own property on his front lawn.

programme.sub-title: Fire Is Coming, Time to Run!

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Officers do a probation search of a known heroin user. A person with two felony warrants has a propensity to evade capture. A motorcyclist is pinned between vehicles after an accident.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies pick up people with PRCS warrants of $150,000 or more.

programme.desc: Detectives and deputies arrest sex traffickers in a prostitution sting operation. A store owner reports a suspicious man looking into car windows. A deputy is called to a business where four people sitting in a car appear to be up to no good.

programme.desc: A troubled young man with physical limitations decides to fight with the police. The boat patrol on Lake Tahoe responds to an overturned vessel in the water. A vehicle with an out-of-date registration is pulled over.

programme.desc: The leader of the Western El Dorado Narcotics Enforcement Team talks about the county's marijuana problem. The team takes down a hidden commercial marijuana grow house.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of domestic abuse along with an assault with a deadly weapon. A traffic stop on a vehicle with an expired registration reveals three women who have some surprising drug habits.
